name: Chinatown Cultural Plaza
tagline: Traditional herbalists, dim sum restaurants, and Maunakea Street lei stands — the living heart of Honolulu's Chinatown.
bestTime: Mornings for the freshest lei on Maunakea St; Chinese New Year for festivals
talkStory: Built in 1973 to anchor Honolulu's Chinese community after waves of redevelopment threatened to displace Chinatown's oldest businesses. The courtyard's feng shui layout and statue of Sun Yat-sen reflect deep ties — Sun Yat-sen attended school in Honolulu and partly planned the Chinese revolution from here. Upstairs herbalists have been mixing traditional remedies for decades using the same suppliers. Hit the Maunakea Street lei stands just outside before 9am for the freshest double-orchid lei at a fraction of airport prices.
